name: @moneyworks/data
description: Intelligent data access layer for MoneyWorks REST API with automatic field discovery
overview: This package provides a sophisticated data access layer featuring automatic field discovery, multiple export formats, and a clean repository pattern. It abstracts the complexities of MoneyWorks' TSV-based REST API while maintaining full type safety and offering flexible data export options.

exports.name: Main Entry Points
exports.description: Primary functions for creating configured clients and repositories

items.name: createDataLayer
items.description: Creates a configured data layer with smart client and repositories. Uses environment variables for configuration if not provided.

items.name: createSmartClient
items.description: Creates a smart client instance with automatic field discovery capabilities

items.name: createRepository
items.description: Creates a generic repository instance for any MoneyWorks table

exports.name: Client Classes
exports.description: Low-level and high-level client implementations for MoneyWorks REST API

items.name: MoneyWorksRESTClient
items.description: Low-level REST client that handles raw TSV/XML communication with MoneyWorks server. Manages authentication and connection pooling.

items.name: SmartMoneyWorksClient
items.description: High-level client with automatic field discovery. Extends REST client to provide typed object responses and multiple export formats.

exports.name: Repository Classes
exports.description: Repository pattern implementations providing clean data access abstraction

items.name: BaseMoneyWorksRepository
items.description: Abstract base class for all entity repositories. Provides standard CRUD operations and can be extended for custom entities.

items.name: TaxRateRepository
items.description: Concrete repository implementation for TaxRate entity with specialized methods

exports.name: Field Discovery
exports.description: Functions for automatic field structure discovery from MoneyWorks tables

items.name: discoverTableStructure
items.description: Discovers field names, types, and order by exporting one record as XML. This is the core innovation that enables automatic field mapping.

items.name: getFieldInfo
items.description: Gets cached field information or discovers it if not cached
items.example:
const fields = await getFieldInfo(client, 'TaxRate');
// Returns cached structure or discovers if needed

items.name: clearFieldCache
items.description: Clears cached field structure for a specific table. Use when table structure changes.

items.name: clearAllFieldCaches
items.description: Clears all cached field structures. Use when upgrading MoneyWorks or after major schema changes.

exports.name: TSV Parsing
exports.description: Utilities for parsing MoneyWorks TSV format with discovered field structures

items.name: parseTSVWithStructure
items.description: Converts TSV arrays to objects using discovered field structure. Handles type conversions for dates and numbers.

items.name: validateTSVStructure
items.description: Validates that a TSV row matches the expected field structure

exports.name: Export Format Converters
exports.description: Functions to convert between different export formats

items.name: convertToCompact
items.description: Converts objects to compact array format for network efficiency

items.name: convertToCompactHeaders
items.description: Converts objects to compact format with header row

items.name: convertToSchema
items.signature: (data: any[], tableName: string, fieldInfo: Record<string, FieldInfo>): SchemaEnrichedExport
items.description: Converts to schema-enriched format with full metadata for LLM understanding

exports.name: Types and Interfaces
exports.description: Core type definitions for configuration, queries, and data structures

items.name: MoneyWorksConfig
items.description: Configuration options for MoneyWorks connection

items.name: MoneyWorksQueryParams
items.description: Query parameters for filtering, sorting, and pagination

items.name: ExportFormat
items.description: Available export format options

items.name: FieldInfo
items.signature: interface FieldInfo { index: number; type: string; xmlName: string; }
items.description: Metadata about a field discovered from XML export

items.name: TableStructure
items.description: Complete field structure for a MoneyWorks table

items.name: ImportOptions
items.description: Options for importing data into MoneyWorks

items.name: ImportResult
items.signature: interface ImportResult { imported: number; errors: number; messages: string[]; }
items.description: Result of an import operation

items.name: SchemaEnrichedExport
items.signature: interface SchemaEnrichedExport { schema: Schema; data: any[]; metadata: Metadata; }
items.description: Export format with complete schema information for LLM consumption

items.name: DataLayer
items.signature: interface DataLayer { client: SmartMoneyWorksClient; repositories: Repositories; }
items.description: Return type of createDataLayer containing client and repositories

exports.name: Repository Interface
exports.description: Standard interface implemented by all repositories

items.name: IMoneyWorksRepository
items.description: Standard repository interface with CRUD operations

importPatterns.description: Import main entry point for creating configured data layer
Import client classes for direct instantiation
Import repository classes for extension or direct use

importPatterns.pattern: import type { MoneyWorksConfig, ExportFormat, ImportResult } from '@moneyworks/data';

designPrinciples.description: The system automatically discovers field structures from MoneyWorks XML exports, eliminating the need for manual field mapping and ensuring compatibility across different MoneyWorks versions.
Supports four distinct export formats (compact, compact-headers, full, schema) to optimize for different use cases - from network efficiency to developer experience to LLM consumption.
Clean abstraction layer that encapsulates data access logic, making it easy to add business methods and maintain consistent data handling across the application.
Leverages TypeScript and branded types from @moneyworks/canonical to ensure compile-time safety and prevent common data type errors.
Field structures are discovered once and cached, providing optimal performance while maintaining flexibility to clear caches when schemas change.

futurePlans.description: Implement repositories for all MoneyWorks entities (Account, Transaction, Name, Product, etc.)
Add GraphQL schema generation from discovered structures
WebSocket support for real-time data updates
Fluent API for building complex MoneyWorks queries
Optimized handling for large-scale import/export operations
